segments.text: I vowed to myself never to look with desire at young women.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What should people expect to receive from God? What reward should the Almighty on high give them?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Isn't it disaster for the wicked and destruction for those who do wrong?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Doesn't God see everything I do—even count every step I take?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Have I lived a deceitful life? Have I been eager to tell lies?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No! Let God weigh me on the scales of his justice and let him discover my integrity.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If I have wandered from God's way, if I have let what I see become my desires,  if there's any stain of sin on my hands,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: then let someone else eat what I have sown, and all that I have grown be uprooted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If a woman has seduced me, or if I have looked for an opportunity to sleep with my neighbor's wife,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: then let my wife serve another, let other men sleep with her.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For that would be wicked, a sin deserving punishment,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: for this sin is like a fire that leads to destruction,  destroying everything I have.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If I had refused to listen to my menservants or maidservants when they brought their complaints to me,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: what would I do when God came to judge me? How would I reply if he investigated me?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Didn't the same God make all of us?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Have I refused to give the poor what they needed, or caused widows to despair?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Have I even eaten just a piece of bread by myself? Haven't I always shared my food with orphans?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: From when I was young I was a father to orphans and took care of widows.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If ever I saw someone needing clothes, the poor without anything to wear,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they always thanked me for the wool clothing that kept them warm.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If I raised my hand to hit an orphan, confident that if it came to court the judges would be on my side,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then let my shoulder be pulled from its joint, my arm wrenched out of its socket.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Since I'm terrified of what punishment God may have in store for me, and because of his majesty, I could never do this.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I put my trust in gold, calling fine gold, ‘My security’?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I delighted in being rich, happy at all my wealth I had gained?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I looked at the sun shining so brilliantly or the moon moving in majesty across the sky
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: and been tempted to secretly worship them by kissing my hand to them in devotion?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: This too would be a sin deserving punishment for it would mean I had denied God above.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I ever been happy when disaster destroyed those who hated me, or celebrated when evil took them down?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have never allowed my mouth to sin by putting a curse on someone's life.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Haven't my family asked, ‘Is there anyone who has not eaten as much as they wanted of his food?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have never let strangers sleep in the street; I have opened my doors to travelers.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I concealed my sins from others, hiding my wrongdoing deep inside me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Was I afraid of what everybody else would think, scared of the contempt families would show me, so that I kept quiet and didn't go outside?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why won't anyone listen to what I'm saying! I'm signing my name to endorse everything I've said.  Let the Almighty answer me. Let my accuser write down what he is charging me with.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would hold them up high;  I would wear them on my head like a crown.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would explain to him everything I'd done; I would hold my head high before him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If my land has cried out against me; if her furrows have wept over me;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I have taken its crops without payment or if I have caused harm to the farmers;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then let thorns grow instead of wheat, and weeds instead of barley.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The words of Job are ended.
